---
title: "l18_activations_interactive"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggiraph)
library(glue)
library(htmltools)
library(fs)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
```

# [4813, 3355, 645, 5544, 595, 10051, 4151, 2100, 4219, 7479, 3836, 4896] 

```{r}
# ─── PARAMETERS & PATHS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

feature         <- 4896  # <- change as needed
activations_csv <- glue("../../data/neuropep_features/feature_{feature}_activations_ALL.csv")
annot_csv       <- "../../data/neuropeptides_human_secretome.csv"  # Entry, Protein_names, Sequence, Go_function
output_dir      <- "../../figures/l18_secretome_activations/"
html_out        <- file.path(output_dir, glue("feature_{feature}_interactive.html"))
dir_create(output_dir, recurse = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# ─── READ ANNOTATIONS (has sequences) ───────────────────────────────────────────

ann <- readr::read_csv(annot_csv, col_types = readr::cols(
  Entry         = readr::col_character(),
  Protein_names = readr::col_character(),
  Sequence      = readr::col_character(),
  Go_function   = readr::col_character()
)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Entry         = stringr::str_trim(Entry),
    # keep only text before any trailing [ ... ]
    Protein_names = Protein_names %>%
      tidyr::replace_na("") %>%
      stringr::str_remove("\\s*\\[.*\\]$") %>%
      stringr::str_trim(),
    Go_function   = tidyr::replace_na(Go_function, ""),
    Sequence      = toupper(tidyr::replace_na(Sequence, ""))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sequence != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(Entry, .keep_all = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# ─── READ ACTIVATIONS & ADD RESIDUES/TITLES ─────────────────────────────────────

activations_df <- read_csv(activations_csv, col_types = cols(
Entry      = col_character(),
position   = col_integer(),
activation = col_double()
)) %>%
mutate(Entry = str_trim(Entry)) %>%
inner_join(ann, by = "Entry") %>%
mutate(
residue       = str_sub(Sequence, position, position),
title_label   = glue("{Entry} — {Protein_names}"),
title_tooltip = glue("{Go_function}")
)

entries_for_plot <- unique(activations_df$Entry)
length(entries_for_plot)

```

```{r}
# ─── PLOTTING FUNCTION (GGIRAPH) ───────────────────────────────────────────────

# Changes:

# • Title is now TWO LINES: "Feature {feature}: {Entry}\n{Protein_names}"

# • Title is moved to the RIGHT and right-aligned (hjust = 1) so it doesn’t get cut off

# • Function attaches a "max_activation" attribute to the returned widget so you can

# sort widgets later by decreasing peak activation:

# widgets <- purrr::map(entries_for_plot, make_profile_girafe)

# peaks   <- sapply(widgets, attr, which = "max_activation")

# widgets <- widgets[order(-peaks)]

make_profile_girafe <- function(entry_id) {
df_e <- dplyr::filter(activations_df, Entry == entry_id)
if (nrow(df_e) == 0) return(NULL)

seq_len <- max(df_e$position, na.rm = TRUE)
y_max   <- max(df_e$activation, na.rm = TRUE)
y_lim   <- ifelse(is.finite(y_max) && y_max > 0, y_max * 1.2, 1)
tick_h  <- y_lim * 0.09

x_by <- dplyr::case_when(
seq_len > 500 ~ 50L,
seq_len > 350 ~ 40L,
seq_len > 100 ~ 20L,
TRUE          ~ 10L
)
x_breaks <- seq(0, seq_len, by = x_by)

# Right-anchored, two-line title: keep it slightly inside the right limit and right-align

ttl <- df_e %>%
dplyr::slice(1) %>%
dplyr::transmute(
x = seq_len * 0.98,  # near the right edge but inside limits
y = y_lim * 1.08,
label   = glue("Feature {feature}: {Entry}\n{Protein_names}"),
tooltip = title_tooltip,
data_id = Entry
)

hover_strips <- df_e %>%
dplyr::transmute(
xmin    = position - 0.5,
xmax    = position + 0.5,
ymin    = -tick_h,
ymax    = 0,
tooltip = glue("Position: {position}\nResidue: {residue}"),
data_id = paste0(Entry, "_", position)
)

p <- ggplot() +
ggiraph::geom_text_interactive(
data = ttl,
aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, tooltip = tooltip, data_id = data_id),
size = 4, inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 1  # right-align title
) +
ggiraph::geom_rect_interactive(
data = hover_strips,
aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax,
tooltip = tooltip, data_id = data_id),
fill = "transparent", color = NA, inherit.aes = FALSE
) +
ggiraph::geom_col_interactive(
data = df_e,
aes(
x = position, y = activation,
tooltip = glue("Position: {position}\nResidue: {residue}\nActivation: {round(activation, 3)}"),
data_id = paste0(Entry, "_", position)
),
fill = "steelblue", color = "gray40", width = 1, linewidth = 0.1, alpha = 0.7
) +
geom_segment(
data = tibble(
x = seq(0.5, seq_len + 0.5, by = 1),
xend = x, y = 0, yend = -tick_h * 0.9
),
aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE
) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
scale_x_continuous(
breaks = x_breaks,
limits = c(0.5, seq_len + 0.5),
expand = expansion(0, 0)
) +
scale_y_continuous(
breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6),
expand = expansion(0, 0)
) +
coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
labs(x = "Residue Position", y = "Activation") +
theme(
plot.margin        = margin(t = 40, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10),
panel.grid.major   = element_blank(),
panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
axis.line.y        = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.2),
axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.2),
axis.ticks.y       = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.2),
axis.text.x        = element_text(color = "black", margin = margin(t = 4)),
axis.text.y        = element_text(color = "black"),
axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 6)),
axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 6)),
panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
)

w <- ggiraph::girafe(
ggobj      = p,
width_svg  = 11,
height_svg = 5,
options    = list(ggiraph::opts_hover(css = "fill-opacity:1;cursor:pointer;"))
)

# Attach peak activation so you can sort widgets later (highest first)

attr(w, "max_activation") <- y_max
w
}


```


```{r}
# ─── BUILD, SORT (by peak activation ↓), & SAVE ALL WIDGETS ─────────────────────

# 1) Build widgets

widgets <- purrr::map(entries_for_plot, make_profile_girafe)
widgets <- widgets[!vapply(widgets, is.null, logical(1))]

# 2) Extract each widget's peak activation (set in make_profile_girafe)

peaks <- vapply(widgets, function(w) {
val <- attr(w, "max_activation")
if (is.null(val) || !is.finite(val)) NA_real_ else as.numeric(val)
}, numeric(1))

# Optional: if you want a stable tie-breaker (e.g., by Entry), collect ids too

ids <- vapply(widgets, function(w) {

# pull the Entry printed in the title stored inside the widget data if present

# If not available, just use an index

id <- attr(w, "data_id")
if (is.null(id)) "" else as.character(id)
}, character(1))

# 3) Order widgets by decreasing peak activation (NAs last)

ord <- order(-peaks, ids, na.last = TRUE)
widgets <- widgets[ord]

# 4) Add spacing and save

spaced_widgets <- lapply(widgets, function(w) {
tags$div(style = "margin: 50px 20px;", w)
})

all_page <- tagList(lapply(spaced_widgets, function(w) list(w, htmltools::br())))
htmltools::save_html(all_page, html_out)
html_out

```
